scenario: generalized anxiety that worsens every evening before bed

turns.user_message: Hey. I don't sleep well and I think it's because my anxiety goes through the roof every night around 8 or 9 PM.
Yeah... I guess what I mean is, I'll never get a full night's sleep again and it'll ruin everything.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
I know rationally that tomorrow will probably be fine like every other day, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
Can I ask you something? Do you think it's normal to feel this way, or am I making it worse for myself?
Sometimes I wonder if this anxiety is actually trying to protect me from something. Is that weird?
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
I had a moment this week where one night this week I fell asleep within twenty minutes and it felt like a miracle. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but the evenings have become something I dread instead of enjoy.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, there was a period in college where I couldn't sleep either and I eventually just crashed. But this time feels different.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I lay in bed for three hours with my heart pounding, going over everything that could go wrong tomorrow.
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I lay in bed for three hours with my heart pounding, going over everything that could go wrong tomorrow.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think maybe tonight will be different, and other days I'm convinced it's just going to be another night of staring at the ceiling.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to lie in the dark without distracting myself and actually face whatever comes up.
I don't know why I'm crying. I just... this is a lot.
I tried to talk about this with my friend but they just don't get it. They think I should just just do yoga or drink chamomile tea.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to lie in the dark without distracting myself and actually face whatever comes up.
Sometimes I wonder if this anxiety is actually trying to protect me from something. Is that weird?
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is I'll never get a full night's sleep again and it'll ruin everything.
Yeah, that's exactly it. And the worst part is I'll never get a full night's sleep again and it'll ruin everything.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I think the real issue isn't sleep — it's that during the day I stay so busy I can't think, and at night all the thoughts I've been running from catch up.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about the fact that I can't even have peace in my own bed. I didn't realize until just now.
Something shifted just now. I feel tired, but honest-tired, not panicky-tired. I don't know why.
I just realized something. The nighttime anxiety isn't random — it's everything I don't let myself feel during the day. I've never thought about it that way before.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
I just realized something. The nighttime anxiety isn't random — it's everything I don't let myself feel during the day. I've never thought about it that way before.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about the fact that I can't even have peace in my own bed. I didn't realize until just now.
What if I can't change? What if this is just who I am? Some people just lie down and sleep. I can't imagine what that's like.
I think the real issue isn't sleep — it's that during the day I stay so busy I can't think, and at night all the thoughts I've been running from catch up.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I think I'm starting to see this differently. Not fixed, but... like there's a path forward.
I'm not going to pretend I'm okay, but I think I understand myself a little better now.
I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
